Article.ArticleTitle: The Impact of Coronary Artery Disease in Patients With Peripheral Artery Disease Undergoing Lower-Extremity Revascularization for Chronic Limb-Threatening Ischemia.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Division of Vascular Medicine, University of Texas Southwestern Medical Center, Dallas (J.A.B.).
Division of Vascular and Interventional Radiology, Fairfax Radiological Consultants & INOVA Fairfax Hospital, VA (A.T.D.).
Division of Vascular and Endovascular Surgery, Boston Medical Center, Boston University Chobanian & Avedisian School of Medicine, MA (A.F.).
Division of Vascular and Endovascular Surgery, Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center, Harvard Medical School, Boston, MA (A.H.).
Vascular Surgery Department, University of Florida College of Medicine, Gainesville (T.H.).
Division of Vascular and Endovascular Surgery, Brigham and Women's Hospital, Harvard Medical School, Boston, MA (M.H., M.T.M., M.E.S., M.B.S.).
Retired from Harvard Medical School, Boston, MA (M.R.J.).
Frederik Meijer Heart and Vascular Institute, Corewell Health, Grand Rapids, MI (V.S.K.).
Division of Vascular Surgery, School of Medicine, University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill (K.L.M.).
Division of Vascular and Endovascular Surgery, Brigham and Women's Hospital, Harvard Medical School, Boston, MA (M.H., M.T.M., M.E.S., M.B.S.).
Section of Vascular Medicine and Intervention, Massachusetts General Hospital, Harvard Medical School, Boston (K.R.).
Division of Vascular and Endovascular Surgery, Brigham and Women's Hospital, Harvard Medical School, Boston, MA (M.H., M.T.M., M.E.S., M.B.S.).
Division of Vascular and Endovascular Surgery, Brigham and Women's Hospital, Harvard Medical School, Boston, MA (M.H., M.T.M., M.E.S., M.B.S.).
Boston University, School of Public Health, MA (G.D.).
Heart and Vascular Center, Dartmouth Hitchcock Medical Center, Geisel School of Medicine at Dartmouth, Lebanon, NH (M.A.C.).

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Dr Beckman reports consulting for JanOne, Medtronic, Merck, Mingsight, Novartis, Regeneron, and Tourmaline. Dr Hussein reports research funding from a Brigham and Women’s Hospital Heart and Vascular Center Faculty Award, a Brigham Department of Surgery Osteen Award, Vascular Therapies, Inc (ACCESS-2 Trial), Humacyte, Inc (V012 Trial), and VenoStent, Inc (SAVE-Fistulas Trial); and consulting for Humacyte, Inc. Dr McGinigle reports research funding from Novo Nordisk Foundation and consulting for Medtronic, Shockwave, and Vascular Technologies. Dr Jaff is a part-time employee of Boston Scientific Corporation. Dr Farber reports receiving grant support from Novo Nordisk Foundation; consulting for Sanifit, LeMaitre, and BioGenCell; and serving on advisory boards for Dialysis-X and iThera Medical. Dr Menard reports support from Janssen. Dr Rosenfield receives income as a consultant or member of a scientific advisory board for Abbott Vascular, Althea Medical, Angiodynamics, Auxetics, Boston Scientific, Contego, InspireMD, Janssen/Johnson & Johnson, Magneto, MedAlliance, Medtronic, Neptune Medical, Philips, Thrombolex, Vantis Vascular, Vasorum, and Vumedi; owns equity or stock options in Access Vascular, Aerami, Althea Medical, Auxetics, Contego, Crossliner, Cruzar Systems, Endospan, Imperative Care/Truvic, Innova Vascular, InspireMD, JanaCare, Magneto, MedAlliance, Neptune Medical, Orchestra, Shockwave, Skydance, Summa Therapeutics, Thrombolex, Vasorum, and Vumedi; serves as a member of the board of directors of the National Pulmonary Embolism Response Team Consortium; and Dr Rosenfield or his institution (on his behalf) receives research grants from National Institutes of Health, Abiomed, Boston Scientific, Novo Nordisk Foundation, Penumbra, and Gettinge-Atrium.
